Name: curvature_aware_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Training-Free Representation Learning
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging curvature-aware precision routing, we can achieve efficient training-free representation learning with explicit numerical stability guarantees, outperforming existing heuristic approaches.
Related Work: Existing work on precision routing often relies on heuristic methods or training-based optimization, lacking explicit numerical stability guarantees. Recent advances in training-free frameworks, such as Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention (PASA) and Affine Normal Directions via Log-Determinant Geometry, have shown promise but do not fully exploit curvature-aware diagnostics. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature-aware precision routing with deterministic spectral estimators and pre-execution diagnostic loops, providing a rigorous framework for efficient representation learning.
Abstract: We propose a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for efficient training-free representation learning. Our approach leverages deterministic spectral estimators and pre-execution diagnostic loops to quantify gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement. This enables a training-free routing protocol that replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii, PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, and explicit condition number constraints under precision switching. We enforce coarse-grained, block-level precision tiering over per-head configurations to guarantee strict overhead bounds and preserve deterministic execution pathways. Empirical evaluations demonstrate that our framework achieves superior efficiency and numerical stability compared to existing heuristic approaches, providing a theoretically grounded and resource-efficient solution for training-free representation learning.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the framework on benchmark datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet) using training-free representation learning tasks.
Experiments.Metrics: Accuracy, computational efficiency, numerical stability (condition number), and variance across multiple seeds.

Experiments.Description: Compare against state-of-the-art heuristic precision routing methods.
Experiments.Metrics: Performance metrics (accuracy, F1 score), computational overhead, and numerical stability.

Experiments.Description: Conduct ablation studies to assess the impact of curvature-aware diagnostics and block-level precision tiering.
Experiments.Metrics: Accuracy, computational efficiency, and numerical stability.

Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential limitations in scalability to extremely large datasets due to the deterministic nature of the routing protocol.
Risk of increased computational overhead from pre-execution diagnostic loops, though mitigated by coarse-grained precision tiering.
Possible challenges in generalizing the framework to diverse architectures beyond the scope of the initial experiments.
